sensory.sight: A thumbnail-sized rectangle of matte-black ceramic, edges chamfered to defeat standard scan geometries, with a single cyan trace line visible only under UV illumination
sensory.sound: Silent until insertion — then a subsonic resonance that settles behind the molars, just below the threshold of conscious hearing
sensory.smell: Ozone and something almost biological, like the interior of a licensed clinic but with an undertone that doesn't belong in anything approved
sensory.vibe: The specific dread of holding an object that makes you worth more dead than alive to at least three parties you can name

expandedDescription: The Ghost Chip Mark IV is the street's answer to corporate identity enforcement — a neural override small enough to swallow and dangerous enough to disappear you if the wrong scanner catches the RF signature. Slot it and your mesh broadcasts whatever identity the corps need to see. The market charges accordingly, and the sellers don't mention the passive uplink baked into every unit's firmware, a dead-man's switch that nobody has triggered yet. In Neo-Tokyo 2099, the distance between liberation technology and a longer leash has always been measured in trust you cannot afford to spend.
